title: lasianthaea ceanothifolia var. gracilis
intro:
Lasianthaea ceanothifolia var. gracilis, also known as the Pacific island celery top or Lasianthaea gracilis, is a plant species in the Asteraceae family that is native to the Pacific Islands and is characterized by its celery-like leaves and stems. The medicinal properties of Lasianthaea ceanothifolia var. gracilis include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions due to its medicinal properties. The herbal preparations of Lasianthaea ceanothifolia var. gracilis include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.

However, I can not verify all the uses.
